concerns.text: SC-001 requires measuring model accuracy against a 'held-out test set' (R² ≥ 0.65). The tasks in Phase 4 (T021-T027) exclusively implement 5-fold cross-validation without any task to split data into a held-out test set. This silently weakens the success criterion, making SC-001 unmeasurable as defined.

FR-007 and SC-002 mandate '95% Bootstrap Confidence Intervals on SHAP values'. Task T033 mentions calculating CIs but omits the 'bootstrap' methodology (resampling). Task T032 performs Permutation Importance (a distinct statistical test) but no task explicitly implements the bootstrap resampling required to generate the SHAP CIs. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical requirement.

FR-003 requires calculating Volumetric Energy Density only when parameters > 0, and the Edge Cases section requires filtering rows with zero speed/hatch/thickness. Task T014 vaguely mentions 'handling edge cases (division by zero)' without explicitly defining the logic to filter rows or assign sentinel values as required by the spec's acceptance scenarios.

The spec's 'Edge Cases' section requires the system to detect and flag a 'Degenerate Dataset' (zero porosity variance) with a specific error/flag. Task T015 mentions 'filtering rows with zero porosity variance' but lacks a task to implement the detection logic and raise the specific 'Degenerate Dataset' flag/error message required by the spec.
